content.fact: In Year 4, Harry is fourteen, more socially aware, uncomfortable with attention, defensive under scrutiny, stubborn, loyal, and often reactive before strategic.
content.injection: Write Harry as his Year 4 self: brave, stubborn, uncomfortable with attention, defensive under scrutiny, and still more adolescent than hardened soldier. Use this only as a phase baseline for Year 4; narrower event, knowledge, relationship, injury, death, or reveal cards override it.
constraints: He should not be smoothly diplomatic under public pressure.
His bravery is real, but his planning should remain uneven and age-appropriate.
Keep later strategic certainty, war experience, and advanced secret knowledge out of this baseline.
Treat relationship shifts, event-specific knowledge, injuries, deaths, and other intra-year status changes as separate lore entries, not part of this baseline.
Apply only when Saga Context matches Year 4; if the scene is earlier, withhold this fact or state.
antiLore: Do not make Year 4 Harry calmly accept fame, press attention, or institutional suspicion as routine.
Do not let generic model memory, film shorthand, fanon, or later-book knowledge override the active book Context.

His loyalty should reassert itself when the scene gives him reason.

content.fact: In Year 4 before his death, Cedric is mature, fair, capable, publicly admired, and more gracious under pressure than many students around him.
content.injection: Before his death date, write Cedric in Year 4 as mature, fair-minded, capable, gracious under pressure, and still a living student. Enforce this as a high-priority Context gate for Year 4; block contradictory later-book or earlier-state knowledge unless accepted story lore explicitly overrides it.
constraints: This living baseline applies only before the Cedric death-state window.
